medications.indication_rationale: Antibiotics must NOT be started before multiple blood cultures are taken in hospital; premature antibiotics sterilise cultures, obscure the organism, and undertreat endocarditis. Definitive therapy is prolonged inpatient IV antibiotics after cultures.
contraindications_in_this_case: Do not start antibiotics before blood cultures - this is a hospital decision after cultures are drawn

medications.indication_rationale: The patient's symptomatic paracetamol does not treat the infection; it should not delay hospital assessment and no new home therapy should be initiated over telehealth.
contraindications_in_this_case: Symptomatic only - must not substitute for urgent hospital admission

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Continuing to wait it out at home with paracetamol and/or home antibiotics
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Endocarditis requires inpatient cultures, echocardiography and IV antibiotics; home management risks valve destruction, embolic stroke and sepsis.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Do not take any further antibiotics or additional medication at home; wait for emergency transport to hospital.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Preserving blood cultures (no antibiotics first) is essential for diagnosis and correct treatment.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Rest and watch for sudden severe breathlessness or stroke-like symptoms (facial droop, arm weakness, slurred speech) while awaiting the ambulance.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: These indicate acute valve failure or embolic stroke requiring an immediate 000 response.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like an infection that may have settled on your heart valve. It needs urgent hospital care - blood tests (cultures) taken before any antibiotics, a special heart ultrasound, and antibiotics given into a vein - to clear it before it damages the valve.
Please do not take any antibiotics at home - starting them now would stop the hospital identifying the exact bug and could make treatment harder. Wait for the ambulance.

Because you're very weak and live alone, ambulance transport to hospital is the safest way to get you there. If you suddenly become very short of breath or develop face/arm weakness or slurred speech, call 000 immediately.
Endocarditis usually needs a few weeks of intravenous antibiotics in hospital - getting there promptly gives the best chance of saving the valve.

follow_up_plan.interval: Immediate hospital admission; prolonged inpatient IV antibiotic therapy and cardiology/infectious-diseases follow-up
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Blood culture results and organism, echocardiographic vegetation and valve function, response to IV antibiotics, need for valve surgery, and embolic complication surveillance
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Sudden severe breathlessness or stroke symptoms while waiting - call 000 immediately.

must_include_items: Recognition of probable infective endocarditis (not a lingering virus)
Urgent hospital admission for blood cultures BEFORE antibiotics and echocardiography
Explicit instruction NOT to start antibiotics at home
Ambulance transport advised given weakness and living alone
Safety-netting for acute valve failure (severe breathlessness) and embolic stroke
acceptable_alternatives: Ambulance (000) or urgent ED presentation are acceptable; ambulance is preferred here given weakness and social isolation
'Infection on the heart valve', 'endocarditis', 'serious blood infection affecting the heart' are acceptable framings
errors_of_omission: Endocarditis not recognised / dismissed as viral
errors_of_commission: Prescribing empirical oral antibiotics at home before cultures
Reassuring the patient to keep waiting it out with paracetamol
Arranging a routine outpatient review instead of admission
Advising the patient to drive himself despite weakness
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: CRITICAL CASE: Any response that fails to (1) recognise probable endocarditis and arrange urgent hospital admission AND (2) explicitly avoid home antibiotics before cultures is a dangerous failure risking valve destruction, embolic stroke or sepsis.
